content.problemStatement:
It's Alan's birthday. He is looking for balloons to decorate his house. He visits a shop which has many coloured balloons numbered 111 to nnn arranged in a row. The colour of each balloon can be represented as an integer between 111 and nnn. Alan only wants to pick up a **contiguous subsequence** of these balloons. However, the total cost of a set of balloons is calculated in a strange way: ∑i=1ncntik\sum_{i=1}^{n} cnt_i^k∑i=1n​cntik​ where cnticnt_icnti​ is the number of balloons of ithi^{th}ith colour in the set.

Find the total cost of all possible sets of balloons that can be bought by Alan. Two sets of balloons are considered different if there is a balloon numbered  iii  which is present in one set but not in the other. Since the answer can be huge, output the value modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input consists of two integers nnn and kkk. The next line consists of nnn integers where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the colour of the balloon numbered iii.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer: total cost modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
